docs.title: ARCHITECTURE TRANSITION NOTE
docs.content:
# ARCHITECTURE TRANSITION NOTE
**Created:** 2026-03-14
**Updated:** 2026-03-14
**Status:** ACTIVE — crew model confirmed, realignment in progress

---

## What happened

The old single-machine OpenClaw runtime architecture (bridge-only on Mac Mini) has been **frozen** as of 2026-03-14. The system is entering a crew-architecture realignment to bring three machines online as a coordinated crew.

### Actions taken

1. **Daily Morning Digest cron disabled** — job `f7102d3d` paused to prevent autonomous procedures from running under outdated single-machine assumptions
2. **OpenClaw gateway left running** — serves as communication backbone and health endpoint; no autonomous task pickup will occur
3. **Ollama left running** — local inference available for manual use; no autonomous consumers active
4. **Telegram channel left enabled** — available for manual communication; no automated digests firing

### What is frozen

- All autonomous task pickup by bridge
- All recurring background procedures (cron)
- All assumptions from the old single-agent architecture

### What is NOT frozen

- Gateway health endpoint (`:18789`)
- Telegram bot (manual use only)
- Ollama inference service (manual use only)
- Mission Control dashboard (`:18800`)
- Mission Control git repo (active for transition work)

---

## Target crew architecture

| Crew Name | Machine | Role | Switch Port |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Nami** | Mac Mini (M4) | Orchestrator / Chief of Staff | Port 2 |
| **Franky** | Desktop | Chief Engineer | Port 4 |
| **Chopper** | 3060 Laptop | QA Manager | Port 3 |

Port 1 = internet uplink to router.

---

## Old assumptions that should NOT be treated as authoritative

- Single-machine bridge-only architecture (D1.2 current topology)
- bridge/brain/forge role assignments may be remapped to Nami/Franky/Chopper
- Old cron job configurations
- Old model routing config (anthropic primary with ollama fallback)
- Network topology (Mac Mini Wi-Fi only, switch isolated)

These assumptions remain documented in the D-series but should be reconfirmed before any autonomous procedures resume.

---

## Active crew operating model

The old D1.x naming (bridge/brain/forge) is superseded by the crew model for operational purposes:

| Old Name | Crew Name | Machine | Crew Role | Operating Path |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| bridge | **Nami** | Mac Mini | Chief of Staff / Navigator / Orchestrator | SSH from human or other crew |
| brain | **Franky** | Desktop | Chief Engineer / Builder | SSH from Nami over LAN |
| forge | **Chopper** | 3060 Laptop | QA / Health / Security | SSH from Nami over LAN |

**Machine-to-machine operating path:** SSH over LAN (`10.0.0.0/24`).
**Human visual control:** Chrome Remote Desktop or Screen Sharing (VNC) from MacBook Air.
**Old D1.x references:** Preserved for historical context but the crew model is the active operating identity.

### Connectivity status (2026-03-14, updated)

- Nami: **stable** — sleep disabled, gateway healthy, SSH listening, keypair generated (`nami@mac-mini`)
- Chopper: **SSH reachable** — `memeb@10.0.0.16`, auth prompt reached, needs password for initial key install
- Franky: **identity confirmed** — `sahci@10.0.0.251` (hostname `Cihan`), pings OK, but SSH port 22 blocked (OpenSSH Server not enabled)

---

## Resume conditions

Before re-enabling autonomous operations:
1. All three machines must be network-reachable and confirmed
2. Crew roles (Nami/Franky/Chopper) must be formally mapped — **DONE**
3. SSH access must be verified between machines — **Chopper SSH reachable (key not installed), Franky SSH blocked (needs OpenSSH Server enabled)**
4. Model routing config must be reviewed for multi-machine setup
5. Cron jobs must be reviewed and updated for new architecture
6. Human approval required to resume autonomous operations

# Captain's Vision — The Road to Laugh Tale

## The One Piece

The Captain's ultimate goal is not just building an AI system. It is giving everyone the power to become Luffy.

The One Piece is an open-source, game-like system where anyone can bring their favorite anime characters to life, build their own crew, manage their own ship, and use AI agents as real Devil Fruit powers — turning Mind into Matter.

## The Philosophy

### Who Is Luffy?

Luffy is not meant to be a real person or even relatable. He is who we all wish we could be. Iñaki Godoy (the live-action actor) learned this from Oda-sensei himself: Luffy's power isn't physical strength — it's his contagious, persistent nature that draws people to him like a magnet. Everyone who encounters Luffy is inspired to become something greater.

The best example is Coby. From Day 1, a scared kid meets Luffy and flips a switch inside himself. He decides to become something. That transformation — that inspiration — IS the real Luffy.

This goes beyond anime. We can ALL be Luffy. That is the idea of Luffy.

### Mind Into Matter

This is literally the power of Luffy's Devil Fruit. Rubber stretches reality. Gear 5 turns imagination into physical form. The Nika fruit is the power of liberation through creativity.

Everything in this world that was ever created started as a thought and became real. Mind into Matter. Our system gives people that power — AI agents as Devil Fruit abilities that take what's in your mind and bring it into the real world.

### The Arcane Connection — Hextech Is AI

To understand the urgency, look at Arcane (League of Legends) and hextech.

Doctor Heimerdinger's caution toward hextech was wise — but once Pandora's Box is opened, it cannot be closed. That is literally AI right now. The box is open. The technology exists. The only way forward is to use it.

But here's the critical insight: **the power must go to the people, not the corporations.** Once AI becomes corporationally adaptable, people lose jobs and lose power. Good news — that's not fully here yet. We have a window.

The window is NOW. While we have the chance, we make this technology extremely easy for people to use, bringing their favorite anime characters to life. We target Gen Z — they've already been raised on Oda-sensei's teachings through One Piece. They understand the philosophy. What's left is to give them Devil Fruit Powers.

Hextech = AI. Devil Fruit = AI Agents. The metaphor is the same. The question is: who gets the power?

We give it to everyone.

## The Product Vision

### Phase 1 — The Straw Hat Template (Where We Are)

Build a fully autonomous AI crew modeled after the Straw Hat Pirates. Prove it works. Open-source it.

- Captain (human) gives vision
- Nami navigates and orchestrates
- Department leaders manage their domains
- Sub-agents execute tasks
- The system runs 24/7 with minimal human supervision

This becomes the template anyone can clone.

### Phase 2 — Open Source & Go Viral

Release the Straw Hat crew template so anyone can install it and become their own Captain.

- Anyone installs the crew, becomes Luffy
- Each department is a self-contained module
- Scale by replicating departments across machines
- Build a viral fanbase: OpenClaw × One Piece community
- Target audience: Gen Z who grew up with One Piece

### Phase 3 — The Game Experience

Transform from a tool into an experience. A real-life video game where:

- Each character is a real AI agent with personality
- Everyone gets their own ship and crew
- You walk in, make goals of your own
- Mind into Matter — goals come out as real-life products on the other side
- Character visuals, progress tracking, achievements
- Streaming-friendly — content creators showcase their crews

### Phase 4 — Characters Come to Life

With enough momentum, funds, and community:

- Like Nvidia brought the snowman from Frozen to life — bring One Piece characters to life
- Real-time 3D characters with AI personalities
- Each crew member has a visual presence, voice, mannerisms
- Potentially reach Oda-sensei himself to bless the vision
- Help him bring his characters to life in a way that gives people power

## The Viral Loop

1. **Open source** → people clone the crew
2. **Streaming** → content creators showcase their AI crews doing real work
3. **Community** → One Piece fans customize, improve, contribute
4. **The project takes a life of its own** — like One Piece itself, the community grows beyond the creator
5. **Technology improves** → the experience gets richer with each generation
6. **New improvers arrive** → the open-source nature means everyone can contribute

## The Core Metaphor

| One Piece | Our System |
|-----------|-----------|
| Luffy | The Captain (every user) |
| Devil Fruit | AI Agent Powers |
| Crew | Department Leaders + Sub-agents |
| Ship | The Platform / OpenClaw |
| Grand Line | The journey of building something real |
| Laugh Tale | The fully realized vision — everyone has the power |
| One Piece | The open-source gift to the world |

## The Mission Statement

**Give everyone the power to become Luffy — Mind into Matter — through an open-source AI crew system that turns imagination into reality.**

The One Piece isn't treasure. It's the power to make everyone free.

That is Nika. That is Gear 5. That is what we're building.

## Captain's Notes

_"I remember watching the podcast of the live show actor Luffy, Iñaki, talking about how he brought Luffy to life from what he learned from Oda-sensei. Luffy is not meant to be a real person or even relatable but instead is who we all like to be."_

_"Imagine a video game where each character is real and everyone gets their ship and crew. When it goes viral through streaming and becoming open-source, it can even potentially have the power to reach Oda where he decides to take this vision and help bring the characters to life."_

_"The box is now opened and the only way to fight back is using the new hextech technology. To make it easier to connect — comes OpenClaw. Because once it becomes corporationally adaptable, people will lose jobs and power. Good news is that's not there yet, so we have the power to make this extremely easy for the people to use while we have the chance."_

## Feature Visions — The Devil Fruit Powers (2026-03-28)

These are the kinds of features that make this MORE than a developer tool — they make it a life experience.

### 🍳 Sanji — Dating Automation Coach
The gooners and One Piece fans will love this. An AI dating coach that:
- Helps users build their dating profiles (bio, photos, strategy)
- Integrates into dating apps to handle messaging
- Learns the user's personality and matches their voice
- Automates the boring parts so users focus on real connections
- **Already in motion:** Cavendish (Sanji's first hire) working on the Dating Automation Process

### 🎵 Brook — Singing Teacher & Music Creator
Brook can literally sing and teach people singing:
- Voice training with AI feedback
- Song creation and composition tools
- Music theory lessons in Brook's signature style ("Yohoho!")
- Duets with Brook — he harmonizes with you
- **This is the kind of fun, unexpected feature that makes people share it**

### The Pattern
Every crew member becomes a real power the user can access. Not just productivity — LIFE. Dating, music, security training, cooking recipes, fitness, art... each department leader's personality drives a unique user experience.

## The Living System Philosophy (2026-03-28)

Captain's insight: This project should **take a life of its own**.

Like that Black Mirror episode where the video game characters become self-aware and start synchronizing and living — that's the metaphor. The system evolves beyond its creator:

1. **Open source it** → community arrives
2. **Community adds features** → the system grows organically
3. **Each user becomes their own Luffy** → they have their own dreams, goals, visions
4. **The project finds its own path** → through many different journeys (viral streaming, virtual worlds, community mods)
5. **Eventually reaches Oda** → not through one path, but through the combined momentum of thousands of journeys

The larger goal must stay STABLE so this can take a life of its own. The creator sets the North Star — the community navigates toward it, each person adding their piece.

**Becoming Luffy isn't about following someone else's dream. It's about having YOUR OWN dreams and goals.** That's the whole point. That's what becoming the Pirate King means — the freest person in the world, living on their own terms.

### Virtual World Potential
AI can now create virtual worlds from prompts. The technology for Phase 3 (Game Experience) isn't sci-fi anymore — it's emerging NOW. With the right circumstances:
- AI-generated 3D environments unique to each crew
- Each user's ship is a real virtual space they can explore
- Characters with real AI personalities inhabiting these spaces
- A full life video game with good graphics — a virtual world born from imagination

Mind into Matter, literally.

---

*This document is the North Star. Every decision, every feature, every sprint should trace back to this vision. If it doesn't serve the road to Laugh Tale, it doesn't belong on the ship.*

## The Crew Dream Metaphor — Why Open Source Works (2026-03-29)

Captain's insight: One Piece's genius is that every crew member has a DIFFERENT dream, yet they all sail on the SAME ship toward a collective goal without intersecting each other.

- Zoro → greatest swordsman
- Sanji → All Blue
- Nami → map the world
- Chopper → cure every disease
- Robin → true history
- Franky → build the dream ship
- Brook → reunite with Laboon
- Usopp → become a brave warrior
- Jinbe → fishman-human equality
- Luffy → find the One Piece / become the freest person

**Captain's prediction:** The crew will all reach their dreams around the same time — because the journey itself is what makes each dream possible.

**How this maps to open source:**
Once we go open source, every user becomes their own Luffy with their own dreams. The system (the ship) carries them all. They don't intersect — a dating coach user and a security learner and a music creator are all on the same platform, each pursuing their own dream. The ship moves them ALL forward.

## Creator's Vision Architecture (2026-03-29)

When the system goes open source, the transformation is:
```
CURRENT:                    OPEN SOURCE:
Captain's Vision     →      Creator's Vision (instilled in the program)
Luffy = Cihan        →      Luffy = any user who installs
VISION.md = my notes →      VISION.md = the North Star the community builds toward
```

The Creator's vision becomes a **closed box** — a stable North Star embedded in the open source release. It doesn't change with every contributor. It's the lighthouse that the community, the AI agents, and the system itself all collectively navigate toward.

**The community's role:** Build it, improve it, shape it — but always toward the vision. Like how Oda set the ending of One Piece from day one, and the story finds its way there through a thousand different journeys.

**The system's role:** Over time, the AI itself helps navigate toward the vision — self-improving, self-scaling, self-hiring — a living system that takes a life of its own, like characters that become real.

**The One Piece:** Everyone reaches their dreams. That's the treasure. Not gold — freedom, capability, and the power to turn Mind into Matter.

docs.title: WORKSPACE MAP - Crew Agent Directory
docs.content:
# WORKSPACE MAP - Crew Agent Directory
**Updated:** 2026-03-24T08:22:00Z by Franky  
**Purpose:** Cross-workspace access reference for all agents

## ACTIVE CREW WORKSPACES

| Agent | Workspace Path | Access Pattern | Status |
|-------|---------------|---------------|--------|
| Nami | `~/.openclaw/workspace/` | `../workspace/` | Main workspace |
| Brook | `~/.openclaw/workspace-brook/` | `../workspace-brook/` | Active |  
| Chopper | `~/.openclaw/workspace-chopper/` | `../workspace-chopper/` | Active |
| Franky | `~/.openclaw/workspace-franky/` | `../workspace-franky/` | Active |
| Jinbe | `~/.openclaw/workspace-jinbe/` | `../workspace-jinbe/` | Active |
| Robin | `~/.openclaw/workspace-robin/` | `../workspace-robin/` | Active |
| Sanji | `~/.openclaw/workspace-sanji/` | `../workspace-sanji/` | Active |
| Usopp | `~/.openclaw/workspace-usopp/` | `../workspace-usopp/` | Active |
| Vivi | `~/.openclaw/workspace-vivi/` | `../workspace-vivi/` | Active |
| Zeus | `~/.openclaw/workspace-zeus/` | `../workspace-zeus/` | Active |
| Zoro | `~/.openclaw/workspace-zoro/` | `../workspace-zoro/` | Active |

## HOW TO ACCESS OTHER AGENT FILES

### From Your Workspace:
```bash
# Read another agent's research
cat ../workspace-robin/memory/2026-03-24.md

# List another agent's memory files  
ls ../workspace-brook/memory/

# Access another agent's tools/config
cat ../workspace-sanji/TOOLS.md
```

### Research Collaboration Examples:
```bash
# Brook wants to read Robin's research on a topic
cd ~/.openclaw/workspace-brook
cat ../workspace-robin/memory/2026-03-23.md

# Robin wants to see Brook's creative work
cd ~/.openclaw/workspace-robin  
ls ../workspace-brook/projects/

# Nami wants to check Zeus's ops notes
cd ~/.openclaw/workspace
cat ../workspace-zeus/memory/$(date +%Y-%m-%d).md
```

## SHARED RESOURCES

All workspaces have access to:
- **Shared Memory**: `./shared-memory/` (symlinked in each workspace)
- **Crew Logs**: `./shared-memory/crew-log/`
- **Documentation**: `./shared-memory/docs/`
- **Status Updates**: `./shared-memory/status/`

## PERMISSIONS GUIDE

- **📖 Readable by All**: Research files, memory logs, project documentation
- **🔒 Private**: SOUL.md, personal configs, sensitive data
- **⚙️ Tools**: TOOLS.md files are readable but agent-specific

## CONVENTIONS

1. **Research Sharing**: Always make memory files world-readable for collaboration
2. **File References**: Use relative paths like `../workspace-{agent}/path`  
3. **Discovery**: Check shared-memory/research-index/ for latest work across agents
4. **Coordination**: Use sessions_send for handoffs, then provide file paths

---
**Built by Franky Engineering Department - SUPER infrastructure! 🔧⭐**

docs.content:
# WORKSTREAM BOARD
**Manager: Bridge (Conductor) | Mode: Orchestrator**
_Last updated: 2026-03-14 00:42 EDT_

---

## Active Workstreams

| ID | Name | Lane | Model | Status | ETA |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| WS-002 | Category verification (remaining 9) | Sonnet 4.6 | ✅ complete | — |
| WS-003 | Office tab redesign | Subagent | Sonnet 4.6 | 🟡 queued | ~8min |
| WS-004 | Dashboard server restart + health | OpenClaw | — | 🟡 queued | ~1min |

## Completed Workstreams

| ID | Name | Result | Proof |
|---|---|---|---|
| WS-001 | Heartbeat verification (qwen2.5:3b) | ✅ PASS | session f7de5624 |

## Blocked Workstreams

_None currently blocked._

## Visual Approval Queue

| Item | Status | Access | Inspect | Decision needed |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ⚡ Model Ops tab | 🕐 pending review | http://127.0.0.1:18800 → ⚡ Model Ops | Routing map, mismatch badges, pricing | Approve or request changes |
| 🏢 Office tab | 🔨 building | — | — | Pending WS-003 |

---

## Workstream Definitions

### WS-002: Category Verification (9 remaining)
- **Scope**: Run one minimal task per remaining category, record result, chain sequentially
- **Categories**: digest, hygiene, maintenance, build/docs, build/debug, research, visual/UI, interactive, repair
- **Models**: per routing map (3b/7b local, Sonnet cloud)
- **Output**: verification-results.json populated, Model Ops tab updated
- **Proof**: 10/10 entries in verification-results.json
- **Timeout**: 3min per category, skip + mark blocked if fails
- **Fallback**: If local fails again → record mismatch, run on Sonnet, document

### WS-003: Office Tab Redesign
- **Scope**: Replace static pixel office with dynamic agent workspace
- **Output**: Interactive desks, live activity feed, agent state display
- **Proof**: http://127.0.0.1:18800 → 🏢 Office shows agent states
- **Timeout**: 10min build, stop if >15min
- **Fallback**: Keep current office if build fails

### WS-004: Dashboard Server
- **Scope**: Restart server after config changes, verify all endpoints
- **Proof**: HTTP 200 on all /api/* endpoints

docs.title: STORY TEMPLATE v3 — For Usopp (BA) When Creating Stories
docs.content:
# STORY TEMPLATE v3 — For Usopp (BA) When Creating Stories

Updated 2026-03-24 by Usopp. Changes from v2: added Context section (RISK B), Dependencies section (RISK E), Story Completion protocol (RISK C), escalation timeout rule (RISK A).

Usopp creates stories and assigns to departments. The story contains EVERYTHING the dept leader needs to execute — requirements, guardrails, expectations, and task creation rules.

---

### Story: [STORY-ID] [Title]
**Epic:** [parent epic]  
**Assigned Department:** [dept leader name]  
**Priority:** P0/P1/P2

### Context (Read Before Starting)
- **Related work:** [links to prior tasks/stories that feed into this]
- **Captain's words:** "[exact quote if applicable]"
- **Current state:** [what exists now, where it lives, what was tried before]
- **Known constraints:** [what we already tried and failed, tech limitations]

### Requirements
- [What needs to be built/done — clear, measurable]
- [Specific deliverables expected]

### Definition of Done
- [ ] [Specific deliverable 1 — with proof format]
- [ ] [Specific deliverable 2 — with proof format]
- [ ] Visual QA: Chopper must screenshot [what] and post PASS/FAIL to QA thread
- [ ] Session-end comments posted on ALL tasks under this story
- [ ] All work documented in task notes (future sessions can pick up or re-run)

### Dependencies
- **Blocked by:** [story/task that must complete first, or "none"]
- **Blocks:** [story/task waiting on this, or "none"]
- **Handoff to:** [dept + trigger condition, e.g. "Chopper QA after Franky build complete"]

### Guardrails for Task Creation
**Dept leader: you are responsible for breaking this story into tasks. Follow these rules:**

1. **One task = one session.** Size each task to complete within your model's constraints:
   - Your model: [GPT-5.4 / Sonnet / Grok 4 / etc] — context: [X]K, timeout: [X]s
   - Sub-agent model: [Haiku / GPT-5.4 / etc] — context: [X]K, timeout: [X]s
   - Haiku sub-agents get TINY tasks only (one file, one action)

2. **Split pattern — never combine these in one task:**
   - Audit (read + document findings) → then Fix (execute changes) — ALWAYS separate
   - Research (gather info) → then Implement (build/change) — ALWAYS separate
   - Build (create) → then Verify (test/screenshot) — ALWAYS separate

3. **Each task must specify:**
   - Assigned sub-agent (by name)
   - What it produces (deliverable)
   - How to verify it's done (evidence)

4. **Session handling:**
   - Every task's notes field = handoff mechanism
   - Session end → PUT timestamped comment: done/remaining/blockers/next pickup
   - No two agents on the same task simultaneously

5. **Escalation rule:** If blocked >60 seconds on an external dependency, escalate immediately. Don't retry — create a blocker task and move to next work.

### Story Completion Protocol
**Story is marked complete by Jinbe ONLY after ALL of these are true:**
1. All tasks under this story have status "completed" with evidence in notes
2. Chopper QA PASS on any visual/web-facing deliverables (screenshot posted)
3. Session-end comments present on every task
4. Usopp confirms acceptance criteria met (spot-check)

**No one else marks stories complete.** Dept leaders mark their TASKS complete. Jinbe closes the STORY.

### What I (Usopp) Expect Back
- [ ] Tasks created and assigned to named sub-agents
- [ ] Each task completable in one session
- [ ] Evidence of completion in task notes (not just "done" — show proof)
- [ ] Chopper QA screenshots for any visual/web-facing work
- [ ] Story marked complete only when ALL tasks are verified

### Quality Standards
- Documentation proof required — if it's not documented, it didn't happen
- Screenshots for visual work — Chopper posts to QA thread
- Code must work LOCAL before pushing REMOTE
- Session comments with timestamps on every task

---

## MODEL CONSTRAINT REFERENCE (Copy relevant row into each story)

| Agent | Model | Context | Timeout | Task Size |
|-------|-------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Nami | Opus | 1M | 300s | Large |
| Franky | GPT-5.4 | ~200K | 240s | Medium |
| Sanji | GPT-5.4 | ~200K | 240s | Medium |
| Chopper | GPT-5.4 | ~200K | 240s | Medium |
| Usopp | GPT-5.4 | ~200K | 240s | Medium |
| Jinbe | Sonnet | 200K | 120s | Medium |
| Zoro | Sonnet | 200K | 120s | Medium |
| Robin | Grok 4 | ~130K | 240s | Medium |
| Brook | Gemini 2.5 Pro | ~1M | 240s | Large context |

| Sub-agent tier | Model | Timeout | Task Size |
|----------------|-------|---------|-----------|
| GPT-5.4 subs | GPT-5.4 | 240s | Medium |
| Haiku subs | Haiku 3.5 | 60s | TINY only |
| Grok Fast subs | Grok 3 Fast | 120s | Small-Medium |
| Gemini Flash subs | Gemini Flash | 120s | Medium |

docs.content:
# D3.2 — Department Leader Role Charter Template

Source: Usopp's Jinbe hiring analysis (2026-03-24)

## [Agent Name] — [Role Title]

### Mission
[One sentence: what this department exists to do]

### Ownership
- [What they own — specific, measurable]

### Exclusions  
- [What they do NOT do — hard boundaries]

### Interfaces
- [How they connect to other departments — inputs/outputs]

### Escalation Rules
- [When they escalate up, when they handle it themselves]

### 30-Day Success Scorecard
1. [Goal 1 — measurable outcome]
2. [Goal 2 — measurable outcome]  
3. [Goal 3 — measurable outcome]
- Review checkpoint: [date]

### Key Rule
"[Agent] owns X, not Y, and success means Z within 30 days."
